nativeRange.habitat: Intertidal and shallow subtidal zones; commonly found in estuaries, lagoons, and sheltered coastal areas on soft substrate or attached to shells and rubble
nativeRange.biotope: Shallow marine and estuarine; intertidal to shallow subtidal, often in nutrient-enriched coastal waters

decorPreferences: open water column or refugium tumbling
rockwork anchor point for holdfast attachment

goodWith: herbivorous tang as live food source

imageCandidates.notes: James St. John, 4000x3000. Sharp, vibrant bright-red graceful redweed against clean white sand; diagnostic bushy cylindrical branching clearly shown with good negative space.
Bob Peterson, 3244x2485. Striking complete deep-crimson clump on wet sand, sharp and whole in frame; terminal branchlets slightly spikier than the other specimens (minor ID caveat).
James St. John, 4000x3000. Dense vibrant red branching mat; sharp, adds variety of form and coloration.

summary: Green Ogo is a fast-growing reef macroalgae sold under four trade names — Green Ogo, Red Spike Sphere, Mocha Spike Sphere, and Mocha Ogo — that are all the same species, Gracilaria tikvahiae. Color varies with light intensity and nutrient levels rather than genetics.
careNotes:
FOUR COLOR MORPHS, ONE SPECIES: Green Ogo, Red Spike Sphere, Mocha Spike Sphere, and Mocha Ogo are all Gracilaria tikvahiae. Color is determined by light intensity and nutrient stress rather than genetic difference. Higher light and higher nutrients tend to promote green coloration, while lower light and lower nutrients tend to produce red, mocha, and brown tones. Aquarists sometimes purchase the same species twice under different trade names without realizing it.

CARE: Gracilaria tikvahiae is generally regarded as one of the most beginner-friendly reef macroalgae available. It does well both in a refugium tumbled gently with moderate flow and as a display specimen in the main tank. It accepts the range of saltwater conditions typical of an established reef system. Supplemental iron, iodine, and trace elements may support growth in nutrient-lean systems, but most established tanks supply sufficient nutrients naturally.

NUTRIENT EXPORT: Green Ogo consumes nitrates and phosphates effectively, and its fast growth rate makes it a useful refugium worker for nutrient control. Growth rate depends on available nutrients and light intensity.

FEEDING: Green Ogo is reef-safe. Trimmings are palatable to tangs and other herbivorous fish and can be offered directly as live food.

Gracilaria species are broadly considered beginner-accessible within the reef-keeping hobby, owing to their tolerance of normal reef parameters.

SIBLING SPECIES IN TRADE: Related Gracilaria species sold separately include Thick Ogo ([sw-algae-003]), Red Ogo (G. parvispora, [sw-macro-007]), Pom Pom (G. hayi, [sw-macro-006]), Red Spaghetti (G. pacifica, [sw-macro-015]), and Monkey Finger (G. salicornia, [sw-macro-014]). As a red or brown alga, this species will MELT at the base if buried in sand — attach to rock via superglue, zip-tie, fishing line, or wedging instead. Sand-bed planting is appropriate only for genera such as Caulerpa, Halimeda, Penicillus, Rhipocephalus, and Udotea, along with some calcified Dasycladales.
breedingNotes: Reproduces readily in the aquarium by fragmentation — broken or trimmed pieces can reattach and grow into new colonies. Gracilaria species also undergo alternation of generations (gametophyte-sporophyte life cycle) in natural settings, but hobbyist propagation is primarily through physical fragmentation.

additional.name: AlgaeBarn — Red Spike Sphere
additional.notes: Same species (G. tikvahiae), red color morph. Water parameters confirmed identical: 72-78°F, dKH 8-12, pH 8.1-8.4, SG 1.023-1.025.

additional.name: AlgaeBarn — Mocha Spike Sphere
additional.notes: Same species (G. tikvahiae), mocha color morph. Temperature range listed as 55-80°F on this page; the broader range is likely a generic macroalgae template and less specific than the 72-78°F listed for the primary green morph. Used for size confirmation (~4 inch max diameter).

additional.name: AlgaeBarn — Mocha Ogo
additional.notes: Same species (G. tikvahiae), mocha ogo morph. Temperature range listed as 60-83°F; pH 7.8-8.4. Wider temperature range on mocha pages likely a generic template. Confirmed SG 1.023-1.025 and dKH 8-12 match other morphs.

additional.name: Wikipedia — Gracilaria (genus)
additional.notes: Confirmed family Gracilariaceae, order Gracilariales. Genus distribution described as warm waters worldwide with diversity center in Western Pacific; G. tikvahiae specifically is a well-documented western Atlantic species from scientific literature.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros general macroalgae care guide
additional.notes: Slice-wide secondary source — provides authoritative Mosaic-wide parameter targets (temp 22-26°C, pH 8.1-8.4, Ca 400-450 ppm, Mg 1250-1350 ppm, NO3 5-20 ppm, PO4 0.02-0.1 ppm) and the 4-method propagation standard. Added retroactively 2026-06-08.
